key_drivers: Dollar softness remains the broadest cross-asset tailwind, with DXY closing at 98.10 and down 0.56% over five sessions.
Volatility conditions improved into the weekend as VIX fell to 17.48, MOVE eased to 65.70, and SOX gained 2.43% on the day.
The latest official EIA data showed a 0.913 million barrel draw in commercial crude inventories, while Hormuz shipping risk remains unresolved.
Policy divergence still favors non-USD FX: the ECB stayed hawkish on inflation risks, the RBA cash rate sits at 4.10%, and the BoJ remains in a normalization regime.

stale_sources: Atlanta Fed GDPNow remained at the 2026-04-09 update of 1.3% for 2026:Q1 during this run.
FRED DFII10 and BAMLH0A0HYM2 are official series with normal publication lag; the latest observations available were 2026-04-16.
World Gold Council ETF-flow evidence is still the March 2026 monthly release published on 2026-04-08.
Eurozone growth was kept at a conservative stable classification because no clearer same-session official PMI page was directly retrieved during this run.
fallbacks_used: CNBC US10YTIP was used as the fallback check for real yields after reviewing the latest FRED DFII10 observation.
Yahoo Finance chart endpoints were used to refresh DXY, VIX, MOVE, SOX, and copper direction in an accessible machine-readable format.
The official RBA Key Economic Indicators Snapshot page was used because the direct RBA monetary-policy hub returned a 403 response.
The official Federal Reserve March 18 statement was used as a FedWatch fallback because the CME FedWatch page returned a 403 response.
